title: Piece Values
summary: Learn what every piece is worth

steps.title: The Approximate Values
steps.text: Knowing piece value is essential for making trades. Pawn = 1. Knight = 3. Bishop = 3 (slightly more in open positions). Rook = 5. Queen = 9. King = priceless. These are approximations — context matters — but they're the starting point of every good trade.

steps.title: Trading Principles
steps.text: Trade equal-value pieces when ahead in material (simplifies to a winning endgame). Avoid trades when behind — keep pieces on the board to create chances. Sacrificing a piece of higher value for a lesser one is a 'sacrifice' — sometimes worth it for a winning attack.

steps.prompt: Which central pawn should you push to claim more space? Play one.
steps.hint: d4 or c4 — both reinforce your center.
